concerns.text: Tasks T203, T204, and T207 mandate experimental validation protocols (Stark-effect spectroscopy, 100-molecule test set) and comparison against physical measurements. This directly violates spec.md Assumptions and FR-011, which explicitly state: 'Physical measurement validation is out of scope... validation will use QM9 quantum calculation reference data as the ground truth standard.' This is a semantic weakening of the project's defined scope and a violation of the Constitution's scope boundaries.

Tasks T205 and T206 require implementing conformational ensembles (multiple low-energy conformers) and hydration state sampling (solvated complexes). This contradicts spec.md Assumptions which explicitly define: 'Single lowest-energy conformer per molecule from QM9 is used' and 'QM9 molecules are gas-phase DFT calculations without explicit solvent; hydration effects are out-of-scope.' These tasks silently relax the defined constraints of the feature.

Tasks T200, T201, and T208 introduce unapproved architectural changes: enforcing specific bond lengths/angles, peptide planarity constraints, and Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) loss terms. Spec.md FR-004 requires a 'lightweight SchNet-style GNN' without these specific geometric constraints. FR-005 defines the training protocol without mentioning PINN. These tasks alter the model architecture beyond the spec's requirements, constituting scope creep and a violation of the defined functional requirements.

Tasks T202 and T209 mandate the inclusion of atomic partial charges (Mulliken/NPA) and per-bond dipole vectors derived from electronegativity as explicit features. Spec.md FR-003 strictly limits 2D descriptors to 'Morgan fingerprints, Coulomb matrices.' Adding these specific physics-derived features without a spec amendment violates the defined scope of the baseline feature extraction.

The entire 'Phase 8: Reviewer Revision' section treats out-of-scope limitations (hydration, ensembles, physical validation) as mandatory implementation tasks. This creates a logical contradiction with the spec.md 'Assumptions' section which explicitly documents these as 'out-of-scope' and 'future work.' Tasks must not silently relax or contradict the spec's explicit scope boundaries.
